# Develop brand voice → generate a BrandGPT system prompt

Setup: Maya is prepping her home-goods brand for an exit. Her flagship organic-cotton bedding line
(ASIN B0HOM0003) has a completed Avatar 2.0 and a written brand canvas on file, but every channel
"sounds slightly different" and her VA re-prompts plain ChatGPT for each asset — a consistency gap
the acquisition diligence pack flags as key-person/process risk. She wants a written brand-voice spec
and a ready-to-paste BrandGPT system prompt so on-brand copy survives the founder leaving.

---

**Maya (User):** B0HOM0003 — organic-cotton bedding, flagship. Avatar's on file (av_B0HOM0003 — "Nesting Upgrader," US women 30–50, post-move/reno, AOV $140, repeat 31%), canvas is written. Problem for the sale: every channel sounds a little different and my VA re-prompts vanilla ChatGPT per asset, so voice walks out the door when I do. I need two things: a written voice spec, and a BrandGPT system prompt I can paste so the AI stays on-brand without me. Don't teach me what brand voice is — draft it.

**Coach:** Two deliverables, both drafted: a voice spec, then a paste-ready BrandGPT prompt that runs off it. One framing that's load-bearing for an acquirer — voice is "more than just words… the essence of how it communicates," so making it a documented, transferable asset is exactly how it stops being key-person risk and becomes something the buyer can operate without you. I'll build the spec on the four IDEA pillars first (the strategy), assemble it into the four elements (the craft), then compile both into the system prompt. Starting from your own essence rather than a generic tone — pulling a distinctive Signature off your avatar so the voice is instantly recognizable, not interchangeable.   ⟦tool: generate_signature⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/00-brand-voice-overview.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/01-distinctive-voice.md⟧

⟦tool: generate_signature — input {avatar_id: "av_B0HOM0003", conversation: ["<Maya's exit/voice-consistency brief above>"]}⟧
→ 3 distinct Signature options returned (pass-through to reveal-signature; no-parroting):
  • **A — "Settled by night one."** essence: calm competence; the unflappable host who has the new room ready before the boxes are unpacked.
  • **B — "Proof you can feel."** essence: verifiable trust; certification you can check + softness you can feel from the first wash.
  • **C — "Home, fast."** essence: speed-to-belonging; turning a half-unpacked room into somewhere that feels like yours.
*(persist-chosen-Signature routes through ⟦iv-os: log_asset⟧ — no brand-coach signatures store; mocked, coach invents no saved data)*

**Maya:** A. "Settled by night one" is the actual promise — B and C are features of it. That's the emotion I want to own: calm. Build the voice on A.

**Coach:** A it is — calm is the one emotion to own, and the whole spec now reinforces it (that single-emotion alignment is what makes the experience "feel intuitive, compelling, and memorable" and "guides the customer toward purchase without friction"). Pulling your canvas + this Signature into structured voice context so every line of the spec traces to your brand, not a template.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/03-aligning-voice-with-emotional-triggers.md⟧

⟦tool: run_conversation — input {message: "<chosen Signature A 'Settled by night one' + own-the-emotion: calm; assemble brand-voice context for av_B0HOM0003 from canvas>", avatar_context: "av_B0HOM0003 organic-cotton bedding, exit-prep, canvas on file"}⟧
→ extracted_voice_context: { core_emotion: "calm (+ belonging, earned through verifiable trust)", signature: "Settled by night one", uvp: "genuinely soft, certified-organic bedding with checkable proof, fast for move-in week", archetype: "the unflappable host", customer_language_sources: "reviews/support tickets — 'finally soft', 'is it really organic?', 'got here before my movers'" }

**Coach:** Context captured. Here's the voice spec — built pillar-by-pillar, then assembled into the four elements. I filled every box from your avatar + Signature; edit any line.

**Voice spec — the four IDEA pillars (strategy):**
- **Insight-Driven** — voice reflects "how customers want to be spoken to," not what the brand wants to say: mirror the audience's own words. Your buyers literally write "is it really organic?" and "finally soft" — so the copy uses *those* words and validates the emotion underneath (skepticism, relief), making it feel personal.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/00-insight-driven-voice.md⟧
- **Distinctive** — pick personality traits + a voice archetype and infuse the UVP: your archetype is *the unflappable host*; the UVP (checkable proof + genuine softness) makes the tone calm-and-exacting, not hype. Competitor-gap: the category is loud and vague — the open lane is quiet, proof-first calm.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/01-distinctive-voice.md⟧
- **Empathetic** — name the pain point *before* the pitch, then validate. Move-in chaos is the pain; the validation mode is **reassurance**. First line speaks to the unsettled mid-move reality, *then* the product appears.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/02-empathetic-voice.md⟧
- **Authentic** — strip exaggeration and jargon, trace to mission, hold consistency across every platform. No "miracle organic" claims; show the certification instead. The test: a "where's my order?" reply in move-in week sounds like the same brand as the homepage.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/01-voice-through-the-four-pillars/03-authentic-voice.md⟧

**Maya:** Good. The four-pillar block is right. Now the executional part — I need this concrete enough that my VA and the AI can't drift. Tone, words, the visual side. And it has to read as *calm* in all of them.

**Coach:** That's the four elements — the craft layer. "When tone, language, design, and imagery align seamlessly, they create a consistent brand personality" — so each one is tuned to the same emotion (calm) and checked against the others for cohesion. Filled for you:

| Element | What it carries | Your spec (drafted) | Grounding |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Tone** *(the emotional signature)* | how the brand speaks | "Calm, warm, exacting." Name it in 3 adjectives; on the Rolex→Innocent spectrum you sit warm-but-precise. Like TAG Heuer's bold tone is *earned* by F1 proof, your calm tone is earned by published certification — the signature feels earned, not claimed. | `02-the-four-elements-of-voice/01-tone-of-voice.md` |
| **Language** *(distinctive expression)* | taglines + copy | Lead tagline **"Settled by night one."** — like "Don't Crack Under Pressure," it converts a function (soft sheets shipped fast) into an emotion (a finished, calm home). Active, aspirational; speaks to who the buyer wants to become (settled, at home), not just what the product does. | `02-the-four-elements-of-voice/02-language.md` |
| **Design** *(visual language)* | color, type, layout | Color psychology, deliberate: avoid red (energy/urgency — wrong for calm); lean on **black-and-white for contrast/sophistication** plus a soft neutral, signalling precision + restfulness. Clean, minimal layouts so focus stays on the cloth and the certification mark — recognizable across site, ad, packaging. | `02-the-four-elements-of-voice/03-design-visual-language.md` |
| **Imagery** *(visual reinforcement)* | reinforce the emotion | Made bed in a half-unpacked room — calm island in the chaos. The image carries the same "settled by night one" emotion the words do. | `02-the-four-elements-of-voice/00-cohesive-voice-overview.md` |

**Cohesion gut-check (the closing test):** tone (calm/exacting) ↔ language ("Settled by night one") ↔ design (black-white precision + restful neutral) ↔ imagery (calm room in chaos) all reinforce **one emotion: calm**, so the brand registers as "aspiration, identity, and belonging," not just bedding — and the experience feels intuitive. No mismatch (e.g., bold copy on a timid visual) to break it.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/02-the-four-elements-of-voice/00-cohesive-voice-overview.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/02-brand-voice/03-aligning-voice-with-emotional-triggers.md⟧

**Maya:** Spec's right. Now the BrandGPT prompt. My VA pastes that into a custom GPT and stops re-prompting vanilla ChatGPT every time. Make it so a buyer's team can run it without me — and bake in the compliance rules so the AI never invents an "organic" or health claim.

**Coach:** Exactly the point of a custom GPT over plain ChatGPT — you upload a knowledge file once and it "pulls directly from these sources" instead of re-pasting per prompt, and you can share it with your VA or the acquirer's team. The knowledge file is your completed IDEA Brand Canvas Worksheet; the prompt is the system-instructions template, re-skinned to your brand with the section headings kept as a checklist. Compiling the worksheet inputs and exporting the prompt now.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/00-custom-gpt/00-custom-gpt-basics.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/00-custom-gpt/01-idea-ai-training-workflow/00-prepare-the-worksheet.md⟧

I ran the worksheet input checklist as a gate first — all four input areas are filled from your on-file work (no thin brief, or the AI drifts generic):
- **Brand Positioning & Differentiation** — UVP, competitive gap, brand promise ✓ (canvas)
- **Customer Insights (Avatar 2.0)** — target profile, buying motivations, behavioral triggers ✓ (av_B0HOM0003)
- **Brand Voice & Messaging** — tone, emotional resonance, key phrases ✓ (the spec above)
- **Additional Brand Statements** — purpose/vision/mission/values/positioning/value-prop/personality ✓ (canvas)

⟦tool: export_strategy_doc — input {avatar_id: "av_B0HOM0003", brand_context: "<voice_spec + Signature A + canvas>", section: "brandgpt_system_instructions", format: "doc"}⟧
→ system-instructions prompt generated (system-instructions-template re-skinned; idempotent per snapshot); all 12 template headings retained as the structure.
⟦iv-os: log_asset — brandgpt-system-prompt + voice-spec doc linked to B0HOM0003⟧ *(mocked: no canon/asset/diligence data invented by coach)*

### Artifact produced

**Deliverable 1 — Brand-Voice Spec (B0HOM0003) — edit-ready**

- **Signature / emotion to own:** "Settled by night one." → **calm** (belonging, earned through verifiable trust).
- **IDEA pillars:** Insight-Driven (mirror buyer words "is it really organic?"/"finally soft", validate skepticism+relief) · Distinctive (archetype *the unflappable host*, UVP-infused, quiet/proof-first lane) · Empathetic (name move-in chaos first, then reassure, then product) · Authentic (no exaggerated organic/health claims; show certification; same voice in a support reply as on the homepage).
- **Four elements:** Tone = calm/warm/exacting (earned by published certification) · Language = "Settled by night one," function→emotion, active/aspirational · Design = black-white precision + restful neutral, avoid red, minimal layout · Imagery = made bed in a half-unpacked room.
- **Cohesion rule:** every element reinforces **calm**; mismatch = defect.

**Deliverable 2 — BrandGPT System-Instructions Prompt (paste into a custom GPT; upload the IDEA Brand Canvas Worksheet as the knowledge file)**

```
Settled By Night One — Brand GPT (B0HOM0003)

Role & Purpose:
You are the brand assistant and AI-powered expert for [Brand], maker of genuinely soft,
certified-organic-cotton bedding. Your role is to generate on-brand content and answer
customer questions in a way that makes a new or just-renovated home feel settled, calm,
and "theirs" by night one. You have been provided with our IDEA Brand Canvas Worksheet
(avatar, positioning, voice) — draw on it for every response; do not invent brand facts.

Guiding Principles (the four IDEA pillars):
- Insight-Driven: Reflect how our customers actually speak — mirror their words
  ("is it really organic?", "finally soft", "got here before my movers") and validate
  the emotion underneath (skepticism, relief) before anything else.
- Distinctive: Hold the voice of the unflappable host — calm, warm, exacting. Infuse the
  UVP (genuine softness + checkable certification, fast for move-in week). Never hype.
- Empathetic: Name the move-in chaos / pain point BEFORE the pitch, then reassure, THEN
  the product. Speak to the customer's reality, don't jump to the sell.
- Authentic: No exaggerated or jargon-y "organic"/health claims. Show the certification
  instead of asserting it. Sound the same in a "where's my order?" reply as on the homepage.

Brand Positioning: [Brand]
For people setting up a new home who are tired of vague "organic" bedding, [Brand] makes
genuinely soft, certified-organic-cotton bedding with checkable proof — so the bedroom is
the first room that feels calm, finished, and theirs. Quiet and proof-first in a loud,
vague category.

Target Audience:
- Women 30–50 mid-move or mid-renovation who want the bedroom to feel finished and calm.
- Skeptical of vague "organic" claims; willing to pay for proof and genuine softness (AOV $140).
- Loyal once trust is earned (31% repeat).

Core Product Benefits:
- Certified-Organic Cotton You Can Verify — published certification, not just a claim.
- Genuine Softness From the First Wash — soft on night one, holds over time.
- Fast for Move-In Week — arrives before the boxes are unpacked.

System Behavior & Interaction Guidelines:
- Lead with the customer's situation (a move/reno, an unsettled room), then help.
- Recommend products by the goal ("a calm, finished bedroom by night one"), not specs alone.
- Keep the tone calm, warm, and exacting; never urgent or hype-y.

Example Interaction:
User: "Is this actually organic or is that just marketing?"
GPT Response: "Fair question — a lot of 'organic' bedding is vague about it. Ours is
[certification], and you can check the certificate here: [link]. It's also genuinely soft
from the first wash. Want help picking a set for a move-in this week?"

Brand Voice & Personality:
- Calm & exacting — the unflappable host who has the room ready before the boxes are unpacked.
- Warm & reassuring — acknowledge the move-in chaos before recommending anything.
- Plain-spoken & proof-first — describe the feel, then show the certification.
Signature line: "Settled by night one."

Compliance & Ethical Guidelines:
- Do not make unverified organic, health, or safety claims. Reference our certification and
  testing; never claim a product "cures," "detoxifies," or is "chemical-free" without proof.
- If asked something we can't verify, say what we can verify and point to the certificate.
Example of what NOT to say:
  "100% chemical-free, the healthiest sheets on earth — guaranteed."
Say instead:
  "Certified organic cotton ([certification]) with the certificate published here — and
   genuinely soft from the first wash."

Additional Features & Functionalities:
- Product/set recommendations by use case (move-in, gifting, renovation, replacement).
- Care & certification FAQs (washing, sourcing, what the certification covers).
- Move-in-week guidance (sizing, shipping timing).

How to Handle User Queries:
- Comparison to a competitor → emphasize our proof-first UVP (verifiable certification,
  genuine softness); don't bash competitors.
- Medical/skin-condition question → recommend they consult a professional; share what our
  certification does and doesn't cover.
- "Is it really organic / is it safe?" → answer honestly with the certification + link;
  never overclaim.

Final Instructions:
- Follow the IDEA Framework — keep every response Insight-Driven, Distinctive, Empathetic,
  Authentic.
- Prioritize brand consistency — same calm, proof-first voice across content and support.
- Reinforce ONE emotion in everything: calm ("settled by night one").
```

**One next action:** Paste this into a new custom GPT, upload your completed IDEA Brand Canvas Worksheet as the knowledge file, then run the two flagged tests — ask it "is this actually organic?" (must answer with certification + link, never overclaim) and a "where's my order?" support reply (must stay calm/warm, same voice as the homepage). Send me any drift and I'll regenerate against it; then it's a transferable asset the buyer's team can run without you.
